Ad Text:
Join a fast-moving Data & AI team that's transforming how financial services operate. This is a genuine greenfield opportunity where you’ll play a key role in designing, developing and deploying scalable machine learning and Generative AI solutions that will have a direct impact across the business

As an AI/ML Engineer, you'll help shape the future of AI at the core of a rapidly evolving business. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex technical problems, thrives in a collaborative environment and wants to help shape the future direction of an internal AI capability.

Salary: $140k-$150k Base + Super + Bonus
Location: Sydney CBD 
Hybrid | 3 days/week in office

What You’ll Do
Designing, building and deploying machine learning models across a range of use cases
Working with both traditional ML techniques (regression, tree-based models, gradient boosting) and deep learning/neural networks
Developing and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) and Generative AI applications
Building scalable ML pipelines and contributing to platform architecture and best practices
Deploying and managing models within AWS using services such as SageMaker and Bedrock
Driving model evaluation, monitoring and continuous improvement processes
Collaborating closely with engineering, data and business stakeholders to deliver production-ready AI solutions
Contributing to MLOps practices including versioning, automated retraining, drift detection and performance monitoring
What You’ll Bring
Strong Python and SQL skills with experience building and deploying ML models in production
Exposure to classical ML, neural networks and Generative AI/LLMs
Understanding of MLOps practices including model monitoring, retraining and versioning
Experience with AWS (preferred), Azure or GCP and cloud-based ML tooling
Familiarity with Snowflake, dbt, SageMaker, Bedrock or similar modern data/AI platforms
Strong communication skills and the ability to thrive in a collaborative, fast-paced environment

Who are we?

At UpGuard, we are replacing manual security bottlenecks with AI-driven precision. Fresh off a US$75M Series C, we are scaling our infrastructure to process 100 billion risk signals daily. This isn’t just growth; it’s a total reimagining of how the world manages cyber risk.

We build the Cyber Risk Posture Management (CRPM) platform that security teams actually love. By integrating security ratings, threat intel, and agentic AI, we empower organisations to stay ahead of an ever evolving attack surface.

We aren’t just building another tool; we’re defining a category. We provide the autonomy to ship world-class technology and the resources to do it at a global scale.

Our Analytics team at UpGuard supports all of our teams to extract key insights from the data that we collect. We extract data from multiple sources and build insightful dashboards that display key metrics and support leaders in decision-making. We also build integrations between systems to collect required data seamlessly. 

Why are we hiring for this role?As our AI & Automation Engineer, you will democratise AI and automation across UpGuard. Your mission is to build critical n8n workflows and develop domain-specific LLMs that make every UpGuardian more productive. The role will report to the Head of A3 (Automation, AI and Analytics). 


What will you do?


Develop production-grade integration and automation solutions - Design and implement production-ready automation workflows that integrate cloud services, internal data sources, and LLM endpoints.

Lead the development and deployment of domain-specific LLMs- Fine-tune lightweight models on internal data from Gong, Notion, BigQuery, and other sources, then make them accessible to teams across the organisation through intuitive interfaces.

Establish best practices and patterns for AI and automation across the company - Create templates, documentation, and training materials that enable non-technical teams to build and deploy their own automation workflows confidently.

Enable widespread adoption - Run hands-on workshops, provide ongoing support, and mentor teammates so anyone at UpGuard can leverage AI and automation tools effectively.

Embed security & governance - Bake privacy, compliance, and cost controls into every workflow and model deployment, ensuring enterprise-grade reliability and data protection.

What will you bring?


Python & SQL: delivered production systems that handle real data and users

Workflow orchestration: hands-on experience with automation platforms (n8n preferred. Experience with Airflow, Zapier, or other similar platforms is still valid)

AI/LLM experience: Demonstrated experience leading AI/ML projects from conception to production

Clear communicator: translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and gather business requirements

Vector/RAG expertise: experience with embeddings, vector databases, or retrieval patterns

Training mindset: created documentation, tutorials, or enabled others to use technical tools

Cloud platform experience: deployed and managed systems on GCP, AWS, or Azure

What’s in it for you?

Monthly Lifestyle subsidy: Use this for financial, physical, and mental well-being 
WFH set-up allowance: To ensure you have the right environment to work in, we will help you get set up within your first 3 months at UpGuard 
$1500 USD annual Learning & Development allowance: To support your career development, all team members will be able to expense development opportunities against this allowance 
Annual leave: PTO plus two additional UpGuardian leave days to give you time to recharge your batteries.
18 weeks paid Parental Leave: Irrespective of parenting role
Personal Leave Allowance: This includes sick & carer’s leave 
Fully remote working environment: While we have physical offices in Sydney & Hobart, we do not mandate compulsory attendance
Top-spec hardware: All team members will be provided with top-spec laptops for their role 
Generative AI subsidy: UpGuard provides paid subscriptions for all team members to access generative AI tools to support their work

Do amazing work 

A career with Viva Energy is more than just a job. It’s a chance to join a group of people dedicated to supporting Australia’s evolving energy and convenience needs. About Viva Energy 

Join a team that values diversity. Viva Energy is a great place to be yourself. We believe in our people, trust in their positive intentions, and empower everyone to amplify their voice across the business. We value inclusion because that’s how we get the best outcomes.

Job Description

Viva Energy is seeking an experienced AI Lead to shape and drive our enterprise AI strategy. This role is responsible for establishing effective operating models, roadmaps, and governance frameworks to ensure the successful adoption of AI across the organisation.

Working closely with both technology and business teams, the AI Lead will architect scalable, secure, and commercially viable AI-driven solutions. This position combines strategic Solution Architecture with hands-on Solution Design, ensuring all AI initiatives align with governance principles, ethical standards, and regulatory requirements.

Responsibilities & Requirements

 

AI Strategy & Leadership

Define and lead the enterprise AI strategy, ensuring alignment with business priorities and delivery of measurable value.

Operating Model & Capability

Design and implement the AI operating model, including clear roles, responsibilities, and governance frameworks to support sustainable AI adoption.

Strategic Roadmaps

Develop and maintain AI roadmaps, frameworks, and reusable patterns to guide technology investment and drive business outcomes.

Security & Compliance

Architect AI solutions that comply with enterprise security standards, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices.

Financial Accountability

Ensure a commercially sound approach to AI by balancing innovation with cost discipline, delivering initiatives within agreed financial targets.

Operational Excellence

Oversee the operationalisation of AI solutions, ensuring they are scalable, maintainable, and provide ongoing value to stakeholders.

AI Governance & Ethics

Define and embed enterprise-wide AI governance frameworks, ensuring alignment with ethical principles, risk management, and compliance standards.

Technical Leadership

Provide leadership and guidance to technology teams, establishing best practices in AI architecture, design, and delivery.

Use Case Delivery

Support the identification, design, and delivery of high-impact AI use cases that drive tangible business outcomes.
Qualifications
Degree in Information Technology or related field (preferred).
AI-related certifications (preferred).
Extensive proven experience as a Solution Architect delivering architecture across enterprise programs.
Strong knowledge of data privacy, security and regulatory requirements.
Additional Information

We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and encourage and support diversity in our traditional and non-traditional roles.

Please note: This role is being sourced through Viva Energy Australia, therefore we will not be accepting applications via external recruitment agencies.

About the program:

AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.



About the role:

We are seeking an experienced RF Engineer (E3 Specialist) to join the AIR6500 Specialty Engineering Team, supporting one of Australia’s most significant integrated air and missile defence programs.

In this senior technical leadership as a Senior RF Engineer – E3 Specialist, you will be the sole authority on all electromagnetic matters for the project and will ensure that every component, subsystem and installation meets the highest EMC/EMI, EMSEC, RADHAZ and co‑site interference standards from inception to acceptance.

You will work closely with engineering teams, program leadership, Defence stakeholders and external partners to deliver safe, secure and compliant capability.




Key responsibilities:

Whole‑system E3 Leadership - Own EMC/EMI, EMSEC, RADHAZ and Co‑site engineering across design, analysis, test and acceptance. Define and deliver the end‑to‑end electromagnetic integrity strategy.

Plan & Artefact Governance - Draft, review and sign‑off E3 plans, CDRLs, technical specifications and E3 test methods for the entire program.

Design & Change Management - Provide expert input in early design reviews, change control, risk assessments and cost–benefit analyses for all RF‑related issues.

Impact Assessment & Issue Resolution - Lead E3 impact investigations, produce mitigation plans, drive corrective actions and persuade stakeholders on trade‑offs.

Integration & Acceptance Oversight - Direct test campaigns, manage test matrixes, verify acceptance criteria, and certify that hardware meets or exceeds all EMI/EMC requirements.

Stakeholder Collaboration - Work hand‑in‑hand with AIR6500 system engineering, hardware engineering & procurement along with industry partners and regulatory bodies to deliver compliant outcomes.

 

About you:

You are a highly experienced E3 specialist who knows what good looks like in complex Defence programs, with the credibility to lead technical decisions, guide teams and influence senior stakeholders.

Essential

Experience and awareness across the RF domains of EMC/EMI, EMSEC, Co-site Analysis and RADHAZ

Strong application of systems engineering processes on complex programs

Experience with configuration management and engineering baselines

Ability and willingness to lead, mentor and grow E3 capability

Australian NV1 security clearance and ability to access Export Controlled material

Desirable

EMSEC experience

Experience with E3 testing, Co-site analysis, RADHAZ and RF design principles

Exposure to Model Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)

Knowledge of Agile Engineering methodologies




Why join us:

Work on a nationally critical Defence capability

Lead E3 outcomes on a complex system-of-systems program

High-impact role with strong technical authority and influence

Collaborate with leading Defence, industry and academic partners

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

Flexible work & Hybrid work options

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs

Catapult is building the future of sports performance technology, with a mission to Unleash the Potential of every athlete and team on earth. We don't just work in the sporting industry; we are actively changing it.  Since 2006, our solutions have been leading the way in sports performance software, science, and data, in a world where 1% can literally mean the difference between winning and losing.

We work with over 5,000+ teams around the world, empowering coaches, managers and trainers in premier teams in the NFL, NBA, NHL, MLS, EPL, AFL, NRL, NCAA and more. We provide the information they need to optimise athletes’ health, game-day readiness, and performance, as well as in-game tactics.  

Catapult is a sports technology company that empowers professional teams to make data-driven decisions. We deliver health, performance, video, and AI insights from the locker room to competitive environments, ensuring every decision is an opportunity to gain an advantage, sharpen performance, and build lasting success. 

WE WANT PEOPLE WHO ARE PASSIONATE ABOUT USING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE TO BUILD AMAZING PRODUCTS


We are looking for an AI Engineer who thinks creatively about AI-assisted development, is still on a steep growth trajectory, and wants to learn AI architecture as their native mental model rather than retrofitting it onto pre-AI-era engineering habits. Based in Melbourne, you will orchestrate AI-assisted workflows against bounded specifications under an opinionated scaffold that catches the bugs experience usually catches. This is a small team with executive sponsorship building both Catapult's customer-facing intelligence platform and the internal tooling that makes Catapult engineering durable. If you operate well within strong structure and have genuine curiosity about how AI architecture is reshaping the engineering discipline, we should talk.

The AI engineering role at Catapult is structurally different from a conventional engineering role: you will not write code line-by-line all day. Your job is to direct AI-assisted workflows against bounded problems, review the output, surface architectural decisions that need escalation upward, and capture learnings back into the team's shared knowledge as work surfaces them.

The work spans:

The customer-facing intelligence platform (knowledge graph, context synthesis pipeline, evaluation infrastructure)
The internal AI tooling (our internal MCPs)
Integration plumbing (auth, ingestion, data flows from all Catapult sources, Cognito-fronted endpoints)
Multi-agent reasoning systems (different AI perspectives debating over the same knowledge base)

WHAT YOU’LL DO 

Query the team's knowledge MCP (Model Context Protocol, the Anthropic standard for AI tools to access external services) for relevant practices and decisions before acting; capture decisions back as work surfaces them.
Produce verified output. Hardware test or end-to-end validation as part of "done." No "looks correct" judgments. We care about the quality of our product deeply.
Help productise multi-agent reasoning: different agent perspectives orchestrated over a shared knowledge base.

WHAT YOU’LL NEED

Required (you won't tick every box -- we're looking for the person, not the checklist):

High trajectory You've grown fast in the last 2 years, demonstrably, with concrete examples.
Creative judgment You can evaluate code or design choices and articulate what's good and bad about them. Show us a piece of code in an interview; we'll ask you to evaluate it, not fix it.
Genuine curiosity about AI architecture Not "uses AI tools." Architectures AI tools into your workflow. You have opinions about how AI architecture is reshaping the engineering discipline, and those opinions are grounded in attempted use rather than reading.
Strong fundamentals in at least one systems language (Go, Rust, C++) and one scripting language (Python, TypeScript) We care about engineering judgment more than language fluency; the scaffold helps you ramp on a new language quickly.
2-5 years professional production engineering experience You have enough experience to have developed your own judgment, but not so much that pre-AI-era patterns are deeply ingrained.

Highly valued (same rule -- we're looking for the person, not the checklist):

Direct experience with AI-assisted coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Aider) deeply enough to have developed your own opinions about their failure modes.
Experience with Neo4j or other graph databases.
Experience with Cognito, OAuth federation, or JWT-based identity flows.
Experience with Go MCP servers, Python data ingestion, or AWS infrastructure.
Familiarity with sports data, sensor systems, or wearable technology. Not required, but helpful.
Open-source contribution history that shows trajectory + creative judgment.

Anti-profile (worth being explicit about):

Engineer whose primary mode is following established methodology rather than thinking creatively about it. Many engineering roles reward rule-following; this one rewards creative judgment within structure.
"Used Cursor once" candidates whose AI-tooling familiarity is shallow. We're looking for genuine architectural curiosity, not exposure.

WHY CATAPULT? 

We have amazing people. We promise you’ll work with some of the most ambitious,  intelligent people in an exciting industry, and do some of the best work of your life.
We encourage our people to engage in constructive, open, and honest communication to make Catapult extraordinary. 
We work in a collaborative yet challenging environment to consistently improve our performance, which in turn impacts our customers' performance.
Our workforce spans more than 20 countries. You'll have the opportunity to work with people from multiple nationalities and cultures, and to build your global awareness.  
We value improvement and development. We are challenging ourselves to continuously grow and become a high-performance company. That means we maintain a growth mindset in everything we do, and our people are always looking for ways to improve.  There is an unlimited opportunity to grow, do more, and do better.

Whether you’re interested in sports or not, you’ll have the satisfaction of knowing your work is supporting some of the most successful teams and athletes on the planet! 

Research shows that while men apply for jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalised groups tend only to apply when they check every box. So if you have what it takes, but don't meet every single point in our job ad, please still get in touch! We would love to have a chat and see if you could be a great addition to our team. We are building the future of sports performance. Our priority is to find the brightest talent who can add to our team culture, actively contribute, and be excited about what they do.

All offers of employment are subject to Catapult's positive prehire check. To find out more, please contact the Talent Partner for this role.

Application Process

Please apply with your CV and cover letter addressing the essential requirements of the role.

If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to contact the NHVR recruitment team on recruitment@nhvr.gov.au.

Successful applicants will be required to complete standard pre-employment checks including a national police and work rights check.

Please Note: We will not require any support from Recruitment Agencies at this time and kindly request no contact is made. Only direct applications will be considered.

We are committed to being an inclusive, diverse and flexible workplace where differences are valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experience and abilities.

We are currently seeking a Senior Data Engineer who is passionate about driving modern data solutions within an industry leading Australian Employer of Choice. This role is permanent, full time.

Joining NTI’s Insights & Analytics team, you’ll play a strategic role in shaping and delivering scalable, secure, and high-performing data infrastructure that underpins NTI’s analytics, actuarial and data science platforms. You’ll lead the design of end-to-end data solutions, influence data platform strategy, and work cross-functionally to deliver trusted, high-quality data assets that enable data-driven decision-making across the business.

Working alongside experienced engineers, architects and analytics professionals, you’ll bring deep technical expertise while mentoring others and championing best practice across data engineering, cloud platforms and data governance — contributing to high-impact initiatives that drive innovation and operational efficiency.

About the role

Day to day, you will:

Lead the design, development and optimisation of scalable data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes across cloud and on-premises platforms

Design and implement robust data architectures that support analytics, reporting and data science use cases

Drive the adoption of modern data engineering practices, including DevOps, CI/CD and automation of data workflows

Develop and maintain high-quality, curated data assets for use across multiple business domains

Optimise data ingestion, transformation and delivery processes to ensure performance, reliability and scalability

Establish and enforce data governance, security and quality standards across the data lifecycle

Collaborate with stakeholders across IT, Insights & Analytics and the wider business to translate complex requirements into technical solutions

Provide technical leadership, mentorship and guidance to data engineers and broader analytics teams

Evaluate and implement emerging tools, technologies and frameworks to continuously improve NTI’s data ecosystem

Create and maintain comprehensive technical documentation for data solutions, architectures and processes

What we would like to see from you

From a personal perspective, we’d love to see:

A strategic mindset with the ability to balance big-picture thinking and hands-on delivery

Strong communication skills, with confidence engaging stakeholders across technical and non-technical audiences

A proactive approach to problem solving, with the ability to navigate complexity and ambiguity

A collaborative mindset and passion for mentoring and uplifting team capability

A continuous improvement focus, with curiosity to explore emerging technologies and practices

From a technical perspective, we’d love to see the following in your application:

Tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology or a related discipline

Extensive experience (7+ years) in data engineering, including enterprise data platforms and cloud environments

Strong expertise in SQL and data modelling, with proven experience in data warehouse design and large-scale data solutions

Hands-on experience with ETL/ELT tools and frameworks (e.g. SSIS, dbt, Airflow, Azure Data Factory or similar)

Proficiency in programming languages such as Python (or similar) for data processing and automation

Demonstrated experience working with cloud data platforms (e.g. Azure, AWS or GCP)

Experience designing and integrating APIs and working with streaming or real-time data solutions (e.g. Kafka or similar)

Strong understanding of data governance, data quality and information security practices

Experience implementing modern data engineering principles, including CI/CD and DevOps practices

Ability to design scalable data architectures and translate business requirements into robust technical solutions

What to expect when you're a part of the NTI family:

Boasting close to 50 years' experience and backed by two of Australia's largest insurers (CGU and Vero), you'll enjoy stability, a great brand reputation and a team of experts to learn from. We are committed to being a Best Employer of the Best People and as such, we offer:

A real and tangible commitment to your development and ongoing study

Competitive annual incentive program of up to 15% of your salary package

Up to 50% discount on a large range of personal insurance products

Access to discounts on over 350 retailers across Australia

The ability to apply for flexible working arrangements (subject to relevant NTI policies) + more!

At NTI, we're committed to providing an environment where our team members feel comfortable to be themselves and valued for their unique perspectives and contributions.

Ready to apply? We can't wait to read your application!

We'd love for you to apply with a resume and cover letter explaining why you'd like to join our team.

Our application process is tailored to each role based on the unique requirements. You may be required to complete phone and face to face interviews, and qualification and reference checks as part of the application process.

Job Title: Senior Manager Advanced Analytics - Data Science and AI
Ad Text:
Your Impact

 

You will define and execute an enterprise-wide strategy spanning advanced analytics, data science, AI and GenAI, ensuring alignment with business priorities, technology direction and long-term value creation. Working in close partnership with senior and executive leaders, you will translate complex analytical and AI concepts into clear, actionable insights that drive stronger decision-making and commercial outcomes. 

 

Key Responsibilities 

 

Define and deliver Asahi’s enterprise strategy and multi-year roadmap for analytics, data science, AI and GenAI 
Lead the AI Centre of Excellence, setting standards and scalable delivery models 
Oversee delivery of high-impact analytics, AI/ML and GenAI solutions 
Partner with senior leaders to shape and prioritise value-led use cases 
Establish and embed governance, responsible AI, risk and compliance frameworks 
Build, lead and develop high-performing multidisciplinary teams and partners 
Uplift enterprise capability through enablement, tooling and operating-model integration 
Manage strategic technology and delivery partners to maximise outcomes 

 

About You 

 

Extensive experience in data science, AI/ML or advanced analytics leadership roles 
Proven track record delivering enterprise-scale AI and GenAI initiatives 
Strong executive stakeholder management with experience influencing senior leadership teams 
Deep expertise across AI/ML, GenAI, cloud platforms (Azure strong preference) and modern data tools 
Experience working with SAP and Service Now highly desired  
Experience leading multidisciplinary teams and building Centres of Excellence 
Strong business acumen with a focus on measurable value and outcomes  
FMCG or complex, fast-paced industry experience highly desirable  

 

Why Asahi Beverages? 

 

At Asahi Beverages, we're passionate about crafting more than just drinks - we craft moments of connection, celebration, and shared enjoyment. We bring together a diverse portfolio of iconic beverage brands, a commitment to quality, and a drive to constantly innovate. Headquartered in Melbourne and with facilities right across Australia and New Zealand, you’ll find Asahi is a place where talent is nurtured, and ambition is rewarded. If you’re ready to toast your future, we’re ready to meet you. 


 
We are a Circle Back Initiative employer and commit to responding to all applicants.

The Role

As an Engineer in the RAN Maritime Warfare Centre (MWC) you will apply and develop your engineering/scientific knowledge to operate and maintain complex systems and analyse data relating to platform signatures and threat susceptibility.

Our team is dedicated to reducing the susceptibility of our fleet to modern threats through the collection and analysis of underwater signatures using a variety of measurement facilities (Ranges). We don’t anticipate that you will start as a subject matter expert, however attention to detail and aptitude in learning data analysis techniques will be beneficial, and we commit to supporting you to develop and enhance your skills. This may include training on naval systems, signature analysis and mathematical modelling. We are also dedicated to fostering your career growth and will provide you opportunities to develop project management, Test & Evaluation and leadership skills which will help you achieve your professional goals.

This position will be required to access US export controlled materiel and therefore applicants will be requested to disclose any dual citizenship with another country during the recruitment process. Dual citizens may be subject to further assessment and appointment to this position will be conditional upon approval to access US controlled export material in accordance with International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR).

This role is eligible for an additional remuneration benefit known in Defence as a Building Defence Capability Payment (BDCP). This enables Defence to provide a premium, in addition to the base salary otherwise payable under the Defence Enterprise Collective Agreement (DECA), for positions in occupational disciplines/classifications that are critical to Defence capability. For further information, please contact the contact officer.

About our Team

The MWC mission is to optimise and improve the warfighting effectiveness and lethality of Australia’s maritime capability. This is achieved through the delivery of highly effective warfare products that support both the Fleet Commander and the Head of Navy Capability in the context of risk based decision making.

The teams you will be working in directly support this mission through test and evaluation activities including:

Developing analysis techniques and implementing them into our Range Systems.

Consolidating our existing Range Systems and subsequent evolution to suit the next generation of RAN Platforms.

Alignment of Signature Analysis techniques with Susceptibility Metrics and Military Standards.

Use and distribute data as the source of truth to inform decision making for the Fleet.

Come join a team which works collaboratively with Engineers from different disciplines, industry and Government, civilian and military, to understand, shape and improve warfighting capabilities across Navy.

Our Ideal Candidate

The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in working for Navy and an ability to apply engineering/analytical skills to maintain and operate Ranges to measure vessel signatures, collect and analyse data and produce detailed written reports to help minimize the susceptibility of our fleet to contemporary threats. You will contribute to a culture of cooperation for development and sharing of knowledge whilst exercising initiative and judgement.

Candidates are sought with interest and aptitude in the following areas:

Programming skills, including: general software development, process automation, databases, GUI’s, and signal processing.
System Engineering skills for solving “multi domain” problems.
Awareness of Defence procurement practices.
Acoustic and Magnetic Signature analysis.
Development and maintenance of Data acquisition and analytical systems and tools for system performance evaluation (e.g. software, hardware and data processing).
Inspection, operation and evaluation of systems on board Naval Vessels in preparation for, and during, trials.

Application Closing Date: Sunday 24 May 2026

For further information please review the job information pack, reference NAVY/01954/26 on https://defencecareers.nga.net.au/?jati=D0C9D0B6-DE4D-DBD4-3C4D-ECDBA2F340DC

Salary: MW EA Level 7.1 + Flexible Working Arrangements
Ad Text:
Who We Are

In Melbourne, water is essential to our way of life.

As caretakers for Melbourne’s water cycle, we care for water, life and land throughout Melbourne: both its people and its biodiversity.

Each time you drink from the tap, flush a toilet or kayak down the Yarra, we’re there. Primed and ready, quietly delivering some of the world’s cleanest water for over five million residents and wildlife that call Melbourne home, just as we have for over 130 years.

If you value making it count, diving in with curiosity, and doing what’s right, you're in the right place.

About The Role

Salary: MW EA 7.1: $115,394 + Super

Reporting to the Team Lead, Asset Information Systems, this full‑time, permanent role sits within the Asset Information Systems team. The Service Owner Asset Information Systems is accountable for the operational performance, reliability and risk management of a defined portfolio of asset information platforms, including CMMS, GIS, LIMS, engineering document management systems, and associated SaaS and cloud services.

The role owns the steady‑state operational lifecycle of these systems, acting as the single point of accountability to ensure services deliver agreed business outcomes while meeting regulatory, security and contractual obligations.

In this role you will:

Own and lead the end‑to‑end operational lifecycle for allocated Asset Information Systems, including operations, change, incident, problem management and continual improvement
Act as the single point of operational accountability, representing assigned services to business stakeholders, IT, vendors and project teams
Ensure operational readiness and safe transition of new or changed systems into support, including documentation, monitoring, access controls and support models
Collaborate with business users, Service Delivery and IT Architecture to ensure services meet performance expectations, standards and business requirements
Manage enhancements, patches, upgrades and configuration changes in line with Melbourne Water standards, engaging IT Architecture where design or integration assurance is required
Contribute operational SME input to projects, upgrades, integrations, data migrations and SaaS implementations
Manage operational vendor performance and contribute to licence, contract and financial management in partnership with Procurement and the Manager
Monitor service performance and KPIs (availability, incidents, vendor SLAs) and report risks and issues through appropriate governance forums
About You
Experience operating and supporting enterprise‑scale applications such as IBM Maximo - required, AutoDesk products, ESRI ArcGIS, LIMS, document management systems and SaaS/cloud platforms preferred.
Demonstrated experience applying ITIL practices across Change, Incident and Problem Management
Strong understanding of application operations, integrations, identity and access management, and cloud/SaaS environments
Proven ability to coordinate across IT, Service Delivery, Architecture teams and external vendors
Experience using Service Management tools such as ServiceNow
Working knowledge of IT security, access controls and regulatory requirements in a critical infrastructure or utility environment
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to translate technical concepts for non‑technical audiences
Ability to manage competing priorities in a complex, high‑pressure operational environment
Obtain AusCheck Clearance. (please see important information below*)

For more information, please review the attached position description: Service Owner Asset Information Systems.pdf

Why Join Us

At Melbourne Water, we care about water and all life that springs from it.

Our people are fuelled by passion, and with safety at the forefront, we have an unwavering sense of purpose, and are on a mission to protect every Melburnian’s way of life, one drop at a time.

We walk the talk when it comes to flexible working – but that’s not all. Our culture of purpose, safety, results, and learning permeates everything we do. We're dedicated to doing what's right, and this commitment extends to the meaningful work we do in a supportive and inclusive culture that encourages you to make the most of your talents.

Our long-standing commitment to Diversity, Inclusion, and Belonging means that Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, LGBTIQ+ individuals, neurodivergent individuals, people living with disability, mature age and young jobseekers from all cultures can apply with confidence knowing they are safe, affirmed, and celebrated.

If you’re curious, action-orientated, outcomes-focused, and care deeply for public resources, community, and the environment, we'd love to receive an application and learn if you could be a great fit.

How To Apply

Click the 'Apply Now' button below and complete our online application form.

Closing date: Friday 29th May at 11:55pm AEST

*Important Information

The Security of Critical Infrastructure (SoCI) Act 2018 requires Melbourne Water, as an operator of critical infrastructure, to prepare a Critical Infrastructure Risk Management Program (CIRMP), which has been undertaken and managed by the Security and Emergency Management Team.

Melbourne Water are required to assess roles across Melbourne Water to determine what roles are critical under the Act. All employees working in a role deemed SOCI critical will require additional background checks via AusCheck. This role is deemed critical and the preferred applicant will be asked to undertake an AusCheck.

The AusCheck process is outlined clearly in this presentation provided by AusCheck CI Critical Worker: Getting Started Guide | Rise 360 (articulate.com).

About the program:

The F-35 Lightning II will deliver the most advanced capabilities and will transition the RAAF into a next-generation net-centric fighter force that can assure the nation's territorial integrity and national security. 

About the role:

You will support one of the world’s most advanced military training ecosystems, ensuring pilots and maintainers are fully prepared for real-world F-35 operations within a globally significant defence capability program.

Key Responsibilities

Maintain high availability of F-35 training devices in line with daily training schedules

Perform scheduled and corrective maintenance on simulation systems in accordance with OEM requirements

Conduct system administration tasks including user account management and Information Assurance support

Troubleshoot hardware, software, and network issues and implement effective technical solutions

Install, verify, and validate system modifications and security updates in coordination with Configuration Management and Defence stakeholders

Support secure configuration and vulnerability management processes

Assist instructors, aircrew, and contractor pilots in the effective use of training devices

Monitor and respond to emerging technical issues impacting simulator performance

Contribute to documentation including plans, procedures, and technical work instructions

About You:

You are technically minded, detail-focused, and comfortable working in a structured, high-security aviation environment. You enjoy problem-solving and have a strong interest in simulation systems, IT infrastructure, or aviation maintenance.

Experience in simulator maintenance, IT systems administration, Avionics, or Electrical/Electronics.

Aviation maintenance or defence technical support background highly regarded

Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills

Familiarity with configuration control, system updates, and security compliance

Ability to work collaboratively with instructors, engineers, and defence stakeholders

Join a high-performance defence aviation program supporting advanced F-35 pilot and maintainer training systems. As a Simulator Technician, you will play a critical role in ensuring the availability, reliability, and security of mission-critical training devices used to prepare aircrew and maintenance personnel.

Working within a complex technical and secure environment, you will be responsible for system administration, maintenance, troubleshooting, and configuration of electro-mechanical and IT-based simulation networks. You will support ongoing training operations by ensuring simulators are fully mission-ready, up to date, and operating in line with strict customer and defence specifications.




How we reward and support you:

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

A variety of work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs

Learning & Development platforms to encourage ongoing self-development

About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

Special Requirements: 

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace:

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. 

This opportunity is based in Williamtown, NSW

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Lockheed Martin supports Equal Employment Opportunity and Diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, harassment free, diverse, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. Diversity and inclusion are the foundation of our culture and reflect our values of doing what’s right, respecting others and performing with excellence. By leveraging our employees’ unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

We welcome and actively encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

Job Title: Platform Engineer Lead - Pipeline migration and AI optimization
Ad Text:
About the Role

Senior, hands-on technical lead for a multi-quarter platform and CI/CD uplift in a regulated Australian financial institution. You will set technical direction, make the trade-off calls, and personally build the foundations the rest of the team works from.

What We're Looking For:

Deep understanding of the end-to-end change lifecycle — local dev toproduction — and where it breaks.
Holistic, systems view across CI/CD, security, observability, IaC, and developer experience.
Strong leadership, initiative and problem-solving skills.
Excellent appreciation of best practices and an eye for balancing security and productivity in a compliance environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Own the platform uplift roadmap end-to-end: sequencing, risks, and stakeholder communication.
Lead the migration from a legacy CI platform to a modern, reusable-workflow model.
Drive consolidation of multiple repositories and toolchains into a unifiedmonorepo and build system.
Design promotion and deployment governance that balances automation with regulated-environment controls.
Embed supply-chain security, vulnerability SLAs, and artifact integrity controls into the delivery pipeline.
Establish DORA metrics, SLOs, and deployment traceability from a low baseline.
Apply AI tooling pragmatically to accelerate engineering and ops productivity.
Coach engineers, build internal champions, and represent the work to security, risk, and leadership.

Required Experience:

Extensive experience in optimizing and securing pipelines for large monorepos and multiple teams.
Production experience with GitOps, multi-environment promotion, and rollback design.
Proficiency in Python, Go and/or Bash scripting.
Operational fluency with AWS, Kubernetes, Docker, and Helm.
Track record leading multi-quarter platform transformations as the technical owner.

Nice to Have

Experience in an APRA-regulated, PCI-scoped, or similar compliance environment.
Experience migrating between SCM platforms at scale.
Skills troubleshooting networking and operating system problems.
Understanding of LLMs, vector databases, and AI-related technology internals.
Experience with SSO, VPNs and other workplace IT system configuration.

About the Role

 As a Data Scientist, you will play a pivotal role in identifying opportunities to leverage data science, machine learning, and GenAI to solve complex business challenges. You will work collaboratively with cross-functional teams to develop, deploy, and monitor advanced data-driven solutions that enhance decision-making and deliver measurable outcomes. This role will also involve close collaboration with, and will report to Gallagher Bassett’s global data science team, ensuring alignment with global strategies and sharing best practices across regions.


Key Responsibilities:

Identify opportunities for data science, machine learning, and GenAI solutions to address business challenges and deliver value at scale.

Develop and implement advanced data science and machine learning models, ensuring alignment with business objectives.

Research and evaluate competing solutions to determine optimal approaches for problem-solving.

Extract, clean, and prepare data for analysis, addressing data quality issues and performing feature engineering.

Collaborate with product managers and team members to design and deploy data science solutions.

Monitor and optimise model performance over time to ensure sustained predictive accuracy.

Develop and evaluate GenAI applications using tools such as LlamaIndex, LangChain, and LLMOps tools like DeepEval.

Create and refine prompts for querying large language models (LLMs) and assess their outputs.

Work with technology partners to build and maintain systems for deploying models and algorithms.

Provide guidance and mentorship to team members, fostering a collaborative and innovative environment.


About You

We are looking for a passionate and experienced Data Scientist who thrives in a collaborative environment and is eager to push the boundaries of innovation.

Essential Skills and Experience:

A degree in Data Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, or a related field (Master’s degree preferred).

Strong understanding of data science and machine learning concepts, methodologies, and evaluation techniques, and working experience within a data science focussed role

Minimum of 5 years of hands-on experience in end-to-end data science projects.

Proficiency in Python and its data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, Scikit-Learn, Catboost, Keras, TensorFlow, PyTorch, NumPy, Matplotlib).

Expertise in SQL for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis.

Proven ability to lead and deliver projects independently, with a track record of mentoring and guiding team members.

Experience working in cloud-based environments, preferably Azure Databricks.

Familiarity with developing business applications using GenAI/LLM technologies and building LLM workflows on platforms like Azure Foundry.

Proficiency in tools such as LlamaIndex, LangChain, and LangGraph.

Strong communication skills to effectively convey complex data science concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

Experience in insurance or claims management is highly desirable.




Why Gallagher Bassett?

At Gallagher Bassett, we pride ourselves on fostering a supportive and inclusive workplace culture.

We offer:

Attractive remuneration packaging & flexible work arrangements including flexible hybrid/Work from Home options

Opportunities for career growth and professional development

A collaborative environment where your contributions are valued

Paid Parental Leave, Reproductive leave, birthday leave and purchased lifestyle leave packages

Collaborative working environment – friendly, flexible and supportive working environment which is built around great team support and rewarding opportunities

A dedication to your wellbeing through our peer support program, corporate health insurance discounts and wellness programs

Staff benefits including additional purchased leave, volunteer leave days, retail discounts and more!


How to Apply

 Apply now to join our team and help us shape the future of insurance and risk management through data-driven insights and cutting-edge technology.

Click on Apply for this Job to submit your application.

To be considered for this opportunity, you must have the right to live and work in Australia at the time of application. Please note that agency applications will not be considered for this position.
